description_long: This is a bulbous, terracotta vase with a wide, flared mouth and a tall, narrow neck adorned with horizontal white lines. The body is rounded and features two small loop handles on opposite sides, suitable for carrying or decorative suspension. One side of the vase displays a geometric motif with spirals and a triangle, bordered by vertical and horizontal incised lines, while the rest of the surface is mostly plain with some subtle color variations and patina. The reddish-brown clay is complemented by lighter, almost cream-colored decorative bands and patterns. The surface appears matte and slightly rough, with visible signs of age and minor surface imperfections. This vessel would be a striking decorative piece in an urban park, plaza, or public garden, evoking a sense of historical or artisanal craftsmanship.
description: A rounded terracotta vase with a flared neck, loop handles, and geometric motifs in white and cream on a reddish-brown body. The surface is matte, slightly rough, and shows signs of age.
description_view_0: The vase's flared neck and one side of the body are visible, showing incised lines and a partial geometric motif.
description_view_1: This view highlights both loop handles and the plain, darker side of the vase, with minimal decoration visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the vase is shown, with one handle and a mostly plain surface, revealing subtle color gradations.
description_view_3: The front view displays the full geometric motif with spirals and a triangle, bordered by incised lines, and both loop handles.
